raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Self-directed commitment and affirmation of mindfulness-based boundary practices",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "A distinct 'self' exists that can be connected to or left",
    "Breath awareness is an effective regulator in difficult moments",
    "People-pleasing typically involves self-disconnection",
    "Self-connection can and should be deliberately prioritized over pleasing others"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Internally coherent; all clauses converge on the single theme of privileging internal awareness and pausing before external accommodation, with no contradictions",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "The speaker experiences clinically relevant distress from automatic people-pleasing or dissociation, possesses sufficient affect-regulation capacity to implement the pauses, and the practices are embedded in a broader therapeutic context that also values appropriate relational engagement",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "The statements are used to justify chronic emotional withdrawal, to avoid necessary conflict or intimacy, or to mask underlying attachment or personality pathology that requires relational rather than purely self-focused intervention"
}
